Title: Overexpression of BAPT and DBTNBT genes in Taxus baccata in vitro cultures to enhance the biotechnological production of paclitaxel

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell suspension cells and culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late side-chain assembly and acyltransfer step of paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Baccatin III and side-chain donor derived from β-phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the C-13 phenylisoserine-related side chain onto baccatin III during late paclitaxel biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate late precursor required for paclitaxel production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased paclitaxel and total taxane production, but less strongly than DBTNBT; elicited BAPT line reached 134.01 mg/L paclitaxel and 181.56 mg/L total taxanes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression via Rhizobium rhizogenes transformation; genomic PCR; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC in root-derived cell suspensions under control and elicited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A transgenic T. baccata line carrying BAPT under CaMV35S was generated and showed strong BAPT transcript accumulation, peaking at 59.72-fold under elicitation at 48 h. 2. BAPT overexpression significantly increased paclitaxel production, especially after coronatine plus methyl-β-cyclodextrin elicitation, reaching 134.01 mg/L paclitaxel at day 16. 3. Compared with DBTNBT overexpression, BAPT overexpression produced a lower and later paclitaxel peak. 4. PAM expression was specifically enhanced in the BAPT line, suggesting coordination with side-chain precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in the BAPT-overexpressing T. baccata cell line; under elicitation peaks at 12 h and 48 h, with strongest expression at 48 h.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dual elicitation with 1 µM coronatine plus 50 mM randomly methylated-β-cyclodextrins in a two-stage cell culture system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower biomass than WT in control conditions; lower paclitaxel yield and later production peak than DBTNBT line
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Endogenous BAPT expression in natural conditions is discussed as being repressed by methylation in the Y-patch promoter region; transformed copy may escape this methylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The overexpressed BAPT transgene was from T. cuspidata, whereas the metabolic effect was measured in T. baccata. In the BAPT line, many pathway genes showed isolated expression peaks rather than gradual sustained induction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering to improve paclitaxel production, especially in combination with elicitation, but inferior to DBTNBT as a single-gene bottleneck-removal strategy.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell suspension cells and culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal benzoylation step of paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final N-benzoylation of 3'-N-debenzoyltaxol to form paclitaxel.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression strongly promoted paclitaxel accumulation and total taxane production; elicited line produced about 308.15 mg/L paclitaxel at day 8 and around 310 mg/L reported in summary, more than fourfold WT.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression via Rhizobium rhizogenes transformation; genomic PCR for 35S-DBTNBT and selectable marker; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC in roots and root-derived cell suspensions under control and elicited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A transgenic T. baccata line carrying DBTNBT under CaMV35S was generated and showed strong DBTNBT transcript accumulation, peaking at 61.93-fold under elicitation at 72 h. 2. DBTNBT overexpression produced the strongest enhancement of paclitaxel and total taxanes among all lines, with an early production maximum at day 8. 3. In elicited cell cultures, the line accumulated 367 mg/L total taxanes and 308.15 mg/L paclitaxel, greatly exceeding WT and BAPT lines. 4. GGPPS, TXS and DBAT showed progressive induction in the DBTNBT line, consistent with broader stimulation of taxane pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in the DBTNBT-overexpressing T. baccata line; constitutively elevated and further induced by elicitation, with maxima at 48-72 h.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dual elicitation with 1 µM coronatine plus 50 mM randomly methylated-β-cyclodextrins in a two-stage cell culture system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elicitation reduced biomass most strongly in the DBTNBT line; however viability remained above 70%
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Endogenous DBTNBT expression in natural conditions is discussed as being repressed by methylation in the Y-patch promoter region; transformed copy may escape this methylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The overexpressed DBTNBT transgene was from T. media, whereas the metabolic effect was measured in T. baccata. The authors infer DBTNBT overexpression may reduce feedback repression on upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highest-value single-gene target in this study for paclitaxel metabolic engineering and biotechnological production, especially when combined with coronatine plus cyclodextrin elicitation.
